text.title: Research Workflows Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Research Workflows methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Workflow mode MUST be one of: market | competitors | pricing | personas | validation | names | pains | niche.
Each workflow step MUST name the methodology slug it executes; no prose-only steps.
rule.rationale: Step without methodology slug forces guesswork at execution time.

rule.statement: Each step MUST carry an acceptance criterion (artefact present + passes validator).
rule.rationale: Without acceptance, executors race ahead and propagate errors.

rule.statement: Plan MUST name the downstream consumer (spec.md / pitch deck / GTM doc) so the executor knows what shape to ship.
rule.rationale: Without consumer, output may not fit the slot it must fill.

rule.statement: Workflow MUST cap at 6 steps; longer workflows should split.
rule.rationale: Beyond 6 steps, drop-off and coordination cost explode.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
